question: Is the image framed as a top-down flat lay photograph?
Is the container a wooden tray lined with fine sand?
Are there exactly seven distinct pottery shards displayed in the tray?
Do the pottery pieces appear as broken fragments (shards) rather than intact, whole vessels?
Is there a shard displaying the turquoise or blue-green glaze characteristic of Ancient Egyptian faience?
Is there a shard displaying the Ancient Greek red-figure technique (reddish figures against a black background)?
Is there a shard representing Chinese Shang Dynasty pottery with a characteristic grey clay body?
Is there a shard exhibiting the rope-pattern impressions ('cord-marked') typical of Japanese Jōmon pottery?
Is there a shard consistent with Mayan pottery, featuring orange-red clay?
Is there a shard consistent with Indus Valley Harappan pottery, featuring red/terracotta coloring?
Are the seven shards visually distinguishable from one another in terms of color, texture, and style (reflecting different civilizations)?
Are the shards large enough to display recognizable decorative motifs or surface characteristics?
Are archaeological soft brushes visible in the image?
Are bamboo picks visible among the tools?
Are the brushes and picks scattered between the pottery shards (rather than grouped separately or outside the tray)?
Is the lighting soft and natural, consistent with an archaeological documentary style?
Is the image free of visible text, labels, or watermarks?
